text.title: RAG Pipeline Design: Retrieval Patterns and Optimization
text.summary: Hybrid search, reranking, HyDE, parent-child chunking, agentic retrieval loops, and metadata filtering patterns.

rule.statement: Use hybrid search (vector + BM25) as the default retrieval strategy. It improves recall@10 by 15-25% over pure vector search alone. Weight vector at 0.6-0.7 and BM25 at 0.3-0.4 as a starting point.
rule.rationale: Pure vector search misses exact-match queries (product codes, proper names, technical terms). BM25 covers lexical precision; vector covers semantic generalization. The hybrid combination handles both query types reliably.

example.code:
# Qdrant native hybrid search from qdrant_client import QdrantClient from qdrant_client.models import SparseVector, NamedSparseVector
results = qdrant.query_points( collection_name="knowledge_base", prefetch=[ {"query": dense_vector, "limit": 20}, # Vector search {"query": SparseVector(indices=..., values=...), "limit": 20}, # BM25 ], query=FusionQuery(fusion=Fusion.RRF), # Reciprocal Rank Fusion limit=10, with_payload=True, )

rule.statement: Apply a cross-encoder reranker after retrieval: retrieve top-20, rerank to top-5. Top-5 after reranking outperforms top-20 without reranking. Use Cohere Rerank API or BGE Reranker locally.
rule.rationale: Embedding models optimize for broad recall; cross-encoders optimize for precision on the specific query. The two-stage approach gets the best of both: fast vector search for candidate generation, slow cross-encoder for precision ranking.

rule.statement: Use HyDE for technical or domain-specific queries where the query vocabulary differs significantly from document vocabulary. Budget for one extra LLM call per query (adds 200-500ms latency).
rule.rationale: HyDE generates a hypothetical answer to the query, then embeds that answer rather than the query itself. The hypothetical answer uses document vocabulary, bridging the vocabulary gap between user queries and stored content.

antipattern.description: Using HyDE for all queries regardless of vocabulary gap.
antipattern.reason: HyDE adds an LLM call per query. For simple, direct queries where vocabulary matches, HyDE provides no recall improvement and adds latency and cost unnecessarily.

rule.statement: Implement iterative retrieval for complex queries: if initial retrieval scores below 0.7 quality threshold, reformulate the query and retry up to 3 times before returning a "no results" response to the user.
rule.rationale: Single-pass retrieval fails when the user's query vocabulary doesn't match document vocabulary. Agentic reformulation using the initial low-quality results as context allows the agent to construct a better query.

text.reference: RAG system prompt with citation and fallback instructions
